senses.definition: having exceptional beauty or excellence

examples.x: The pianist's performance of the concerto was sublime, leaving the audience in awe of her talent.
As the sun set over the horizon, painting the sky in hues of pink and gold, the view from the mountaintop was truly sublime.
The sublime craftsmanship of the sculpture made it a masterpiece of art.
Watching the ballet performance, with its graceful movements and captivating music, was an experience of sublime artistry.
The sublime tranquility of the forest was a welcome escape from the hustle and bustle of city life.

senses.definition: not exceeded by anything or anyone else

examples.x: The chef's culinary skills were unsurpassed, creating dishes that delighted the most discerning palates.
The athlete's dedication to training led to unsurpassed performance, setting records that stood unbroken for years.
The artist's mastery of the medium resulted in unsurpassed works of art that captivated viewers worldwide.
The company's commitment to quality produced an unsurpassed product, earning it a reputation for excellence.
Her unsurpassed knowledge of the subject made her the go-to expert in the academic community.

senses.definition: evoking a feeling of great respect, admiration, and sometimes fear, due to their impressive or majestic nature

examples.x: The awe-inspiring view from the mountaintop left us speechless.
They marveled at the awe-inspiring architecture of the ancient cathedral.
The explorers were in awe of the awe-inspiring natural wonders they discovered.
She felt an awe-inspiring sense of accomplishment after reaching her goal.
He became silent, overwhelmed by the awe-inspiring beauty of the night sky.

senses.definition: extremely impressive or stunning

examples.x: Her dazzling performance on the stage left the audience in awe, applauding the singer's vocal prowess.

senses.definition: falling below the expected or desired level of quality, performance, or standard

examples.x: The quality of the restaurant's food was subpar, leading to negative reviews from customers.
The team's performance in the last game was subpar, resulting in a loss.
Her grades in the class were subpar compared to her usual high standards.
The hotel received complaints about its subpar accommodations, prompting management to make improvements.
The new product's sales were subpar, falling short of the company's expectations.

senses.definition: extremely bad or unacceptable in quality or nature

examples.x: I can't believe how atrocious this translation is; it barely resembles the original text.
His taste in fashion was simply atrocious, with clashing colors and mismatched patterns.
The first draft of his essay was atrocious, filled with grammatical errors.

senses.definition: dull and without innovation or change

examples.x: Her lackluster presentation did not leave a lasting impression on the audience.
Despite the company's promises of a revolutionary product, the launch turned out to be lackluster, offering nothing new to the market.
The lackluster response to the new product release was disappointing for the company.
The team's performance in the championship game was lackluster, lacking the energy and enthusiasm needed to secure a victory.
The lackluster effort put into the project resulted in mediocre results.

senses.definition: having poor quality or being unpleasant in some way

examples.x: The hotel room had a crummy bed and outdated furniture, making the stay far from enjoyable.
Despite the high expectations, the restaurant served crummy food that left diners disappointed.
The old car had a crummy engine that frequently broke down, causing frustration for the owner.
The students complained about the crummy textbooks, which were outdated and in poor condition.
The apartment had a crummy heating system, leaving tenants freezing during the winter months.

senses.definition: very low quality or unpleasant

examples.x: His lousy attitude towards his coworkers created tension in the workplace.
The lousy performance of the team disappointed their fans.
The lousy service at the restaurant left customers dissatisfied.
The lousy quality of the product did not justify its high price.
The lousy weather ruined our plans for a picnic.

senses.definition: very poor in quality, condition, or value

examples.x: He bought a used car, but it turned out to be in wretched condition, constantly breaking down.
She found herself living in a wretched apartment, with leaky ceilings and broken appliances.
The play received wretched reviews from critics, who described it as amateurish and uninspired.

senses.definition: standing out due to its importance or relevance
not damaged or weakened, remaining in a perfect or complete state without any loss of function or quality

examples.x: Despite his advanced age, his memory remains unimpaired.
The accident left her physically unharmed and unimpaired in her ability to perform her job.
His vision is unimpaired, allowing him to see clearly without the need for glasses.

senses.definition: of poor quality or craftmanship

examples.x: The shoddy construction of the cheap furniture became evident when it started falling apart after just a few weeks of use.
The company faced criticism for its shoddy customer service, with numerous complaints about unresponsiveness and unhelpful staff.
Despite the enticing price, the smartphone proved to be shoddy, with a fragile screen and frequent technical issues.
The homeowners regretted hiring the contractor when they discovered the shoddy workmanship, leading to leaks and structural problems.
The novel was criticized for its shoddy plot development and poorly written dialogue, disappointing readers.

senses.definition: having a lively, energetic, and resilient quality
strongly desired by many people

examples.x: The coveted award was presented to the top performer of the year.
The coveted trophy was awarded to the winning team of the championship.
Her coveted position as CEO of the company was the result of years of hard work and dedication.
The designer handbag was a coveted accessory among fashion enthusiasts.
The coveted internship at the prestigious law firm was highly competitive, with applicants from top universities around the country.

senses.definition: representing the essential qualities, characteristics, or principles associated with a particular type or category

examples.x: This artist's painting is exemplary of the Impressionist style, capturing light and atmosphere with loose brushstrokes.
My documentary provides an exemplary portrayal of rural life, showcasing the challenges and beauty of agricultural communities.
The invention of the telephone by Alexander Graham Bell is an exemplary illustration of groundbreaking technological innovation.

senses.definition: surpassing ordinary limits and reaching a level of exceptional excellence or greatness

examples.x: The painting captured a sense of transcendent beauty that left viewers speechless.
Her voice had a transcendent quality that seemed to lift the audience to another realm.
Love has the power to create a transcendent connection between two souls.
The music had a transcendent effect, transporting listeners to a state of profound peace.

senses.definition: of excellent quality or high standard

examples.x: The company's sterling reputation for honesty and integrity contributed to its success in the market.

senses.definition: beyond comparison or unmatched in excellence
meeting a certain standard or expectation

examples.x: He knew he had to study hard to get his grades up to snuff if he wanted to pass the exam.
The new employee's performance wasn't up to snuff, so she received additional training.
The restaurant had high standards for its food quality, and anything less than perfect wasn't up to snuff.
After some practice, her presentation skills were finally up to snuff for the big meeting.
The company required all employees to be up to snuff on safety protocols to prevent accidents in the workplace.

senses.definition: average in quality and not meeting the standards of excellence

examples.x: She wasn't satisfied with her mediocre performance and aimed to improve.
The hotel was clean and adequate, but the service was just mediocre.
The novel had a promising start but delivered a mediocre plot in the end.
The company’s products are known for being mediocre but affordable.
The team’s mediocre performance cost them a spot in the finals.
